text: {"is_complete": false, "next_step": {"type": "select", "prompt_text": "Last big check before submission: does your project involve human subjects? This decides whether you need ethics review (4–8 week turnaround) — and starting data collection before approval is grounds for project failure under the University Research Ethics Code.", "config": {"options": [{"value": "none", "label": "No human subjects", "description": "Pure software / algorithmic work, no user studies or surveys. Skip ethics review."}, {"value": "low_risk", "label": "Low-risk (anonymous survey)", "description": "Anonymous survey of UM students or similar. Expedited 2-week review pathway."}, {"value": "medium_risk", "label": "Medium-risk (interviews, identifiable data)", "description": "Interviews, observations, or any identifying personal data. Standard 4-week review."}, {"value": "high_risk", "label": "High-risk (medical/vulnerable)", "description": "Medical data, vulnerable populations (minors, patients). Full board review, 6–8 weeks."}]}}, "reasoning": "Ethics declaration is required if the project involves human subjects, personal data collection, or vulnerable populations. The pathway depends on risk level — getting the timing wrong is one of the most common FYP pitfalls.", "running_summary": "FYP I, AD category, supervisor confirmed.", "citations": ["Ethics Review", "Common Pitfalls"]}
